result:
The fix is complete. Here's a summary of the change:

## Root Cause

The `hasCustomGetter()` method in `PropertyNamingRule.kt` was checking if the **first child** of the `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` node had text "get":

```kotlin
findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
    ?.firstChildNode
    ?.text == "get"
```

When a property accessor has an annotation (like `@SuppressLint("WrongConstant")`) or a visibility modifier (like `private`) before the `get` keyword, the first child of the `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` node is a `MODIFIER_LIST`, not the "get" keyword. This caused `hasCustomGetter()` to return `false`, so the rule would incorrectly flag the property as needing screaming snake case.

## Fix

Changed `hasCustomGetter()` to use `findChildByType(GET_KEYWORD)` instead of `firstChildNode?.text == "get"`. This reliably detects the getter regardless of whether annotations or modifiers precede it:

```kotlin
findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
    ?.findChildByType(GET_KEYWORD) != null
```

This is more robust because `findChildByType` searches through all direct children rather than only checking the first one, and it uses the proper `GET_KEYWORD` element type rather than text comparison.
